jokes.text: This is a scam. We're on a nature walk!
jokes.setup: Bart gets excited about exploring a cave with rocks and minerals
jokes.punchline: His sudden realization that it's just educational nature content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - his horror at discovering education disguised as fun. Well-structured misdirection with strong character voice.

jokes.text: Don't worry, Bart. I brought something that'll keep you interested-- a nickel in a water bottle!
jokes.setup: Homer tries to console disappointed Bart
jokes.punchline: His idea of entertainment is watching a nickel move in water
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - completely sincere about this terrible entertainment. The specificity of 'nickel in a water bottle' makes it memorable.

jokes.text: He'll sleep tonight. Where'd it go?! where'd it go?! It's in the cap.
jokes.setup: Homer confidently predicts the nickel will keep Bart entertained
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately loses the nickel and panics, Homer calmly explains location
jokes.explanation: Nice payoff to the nickel setup. Homer's matter-of-fact response to Bart's panic works well.

jokes.text: Local Anahoopi indians believed this stalactite was the finger of Tsisnajini, their god of pointing down.
jokes.setup: Tour guide shares Native American mythology about cave formation
jokes.punchline: The god's incredibly mundane divine purpose - pointing down
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of spiritual reverence. 'God of pointing down' is beautifully absurd and specific.

jokes.text: Silly indians. Our god made their god.
jokes.setup: Learning about Native American spiritual beliefs
jokes.punchline: Homer's casually dismissive and theologically confused response
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer ignorance delivered with complete confidence. The theological hierarchy logic is perfectly stupid.

jokes.text: It's so impressive. I'm just gonna get a tiny chip to put on my desk at work.
jokes.setup: Homer expresses admiration for the cave formation
jokes.punchline: Immediately decides to vandalize it for office decor
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer contradiction - appreciation immediately followed by destruction. The casual 'tiny chip' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Save me, Tsisnajini!
jokes.setup: Homer gets stuck after trying to chip the stalactite
jokes.punchline: Calls on the Native American god he just dismissed
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Homer's hypocrisy and desperation. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: I'm stuck! And I have to pee. Now I'm just stuck.
jokes.setup: Homer is trapped in the cave
jokes.punchline: Adds embarrassing bodily function, then acts like the problem resolved itself
jokes.explanation: The false resolution of 'now I'm just stuck' after mentioning the pee situation is nicely done.

jokes.text: We'll get that fireman who cut you out of that teacup ride. That was two firemen, a crane and snow white.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions getting the fireman who helped Homer before
jokes.punchline: Homer's correction reveals the absurd scale of his previous rescue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that builds Homer's history of getting stuck. Snow White is the perfect absurd detail.

jokes.text: That was two firemen, a crane and snow white.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions the fireman who helped before
jokes.punchline: It actually required much more elaborate rescue including Disney character
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with absurd Snow White inclusion - implies Homer got stuck at Disney

jokes.text: I hate to be alone! Except when I'm watching TV, then leave me alone. But right now, don't leave!
jokes.setup: Homer doesn't want to be left alone in the cave
jokes.punchline: His contradictory specifications about when he wants solitude
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's specific neuroses and contradictions.

jokes.text: Hello, Mr. Bighorn sheep! I've read about you. You're shy, and you rarely approach humans.
jokes.setup: Lisa encounters a bighorn sheep and recites facts about their shy nature
jokes.punchline: The sheep is clearly not behaving shyly
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for Lisa's book knowledge failing her. Her formal approach to the sheep is endearing.

jokes.text: And of course you'd never attack a fellow herbivore!
jokes.setup: Lisa continues explaining why she's safe from the sheep
jokes.punchline: Her logical assumption is about to be proven very wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the sheep attack. Lisa's confidence in her knowledge makes the reversal funnier.

jokes.text: Mad beast! Liberal midget!
jokes.setup: Burns encounters the attacking sheep
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific insults toward the animal
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Burns - turning an animal attack into political commentary. 'Liberal midget' is bizarrely specific and perfect.

jokes.text: Good lord! A wooly bully!
jokes.setup: Burns continues reacting to the sheep attack
jokes.punchline: References the song 'Wooly Bully' while describing the sheep
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits Burns' tendency toward dated references.

jokes.text: Enter password. Damn it! I forget! Forgot password? Enter place of birth. Pangaea. Correct.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to access his mansion's security system
jokes.punchline: His birthplace is listed as the prehistoric supercontinent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant running gag about Burns' extreme age. Pangaea is perfectly absurd and scientifically accurate to the joke.

jokes.text: So long, sucker! I mean, don't leave an old man to die.
jokes.setup: Burns initially appears to abandon Lisa and Bart
jokes.punchline: Immediately corrects himself to sound sympathetic
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Burns' self-serving nature barely disguised by fake concern.

jokes.text: No, he's out getting a spray-on tan. He comes back orange and stains the furniture. The man's a walking creamsicle!
jokes.setup: Explaining why Smithers can't help
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of Smithers' fake tan mishaps
jokes.explanation: 'Walking creamsicle' is a perfect visual metaphor that elevates the joke beyond a simple tan reference.

jokes.text: Wait, wait, wait, wait... Now you're telling me Burns's story? Yeah it's like the play within a play in Hamlet.
jokes.setup: Bart realizes the nested story structure is getting complicated
jokes.punchline: Lisa compares it to classic literature
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-commentary on the episode's complex nested structure. Lisa's literary reference is in character.

jokes.text: Let's see... it's like when you watch old home movies of you watching TV.
jokes.setup: Lisa tries to find a simpler analogy for the story structure
jokes.punchline: Her alternative explanation is even more confusing
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd recursive explanation that's somehow both relatable and nonsensical.

jokes.text: Who's this dried-up ol' apricot?
jokes.setup: Rich Texan sees Mr. Burns for the first time
jokes.punchline: Describes Burns as a dried fruit
jokes.explanation: 'Dried-up ol' apricot' is a vivid and accurate description that establishes the Texan's colorful speech.

jokes.text: I make money using my brain, not sticking a pole in the ground and praying for goo.
jokes.setup: Burns explains his nuclear business to the oil-wealthy Texan
jokes.punchline: Reduces oil drilling to its most primitive description
jokes.explanation: 'Praying for goo' perfectly captures Burns' condescension while being surprisingly accurate about oil drilling.

jokes.text: You need to get your hands dirty, poindexter! Dip 'em in a little Texas crude!
jokes.setup: Rich Texan responds to Burns' intellectual superiority
jokes.punchline: Suggests literally putting hands in oil as a solution
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing the Texan's anti-intellectual, hands-on philosophy.

jokes.text: Down in Houston, we call that a fort worth shampoo!
jokes.setup: Texan continues promoting getting oil on your hands
jokes.punchline: Claims Texas has a specific term for this gross practice
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd regional terminology that sounds believable for Texas oil culture.

jokes.text: At my age, that could kill me! How about we settle things with a scavenger hunt?
jokes.setup: Burns is challenged to a duel with bullets
jokes.punchline: His alternative is the least violent competition imaginable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of masculine duel tropes. Scavenger hunt is the perfect wimpy Burns alternative.

jokes.text: I'm gonna win me a nucular plant! Dream on, bitch.
jokes.setup: The stakes of the scavenger hunt are established
jokes.punchline: Burns' surprisingly harsh trash talk
jokes.explanation: Burns dropping a casual 'bitch' is unexpected and funny contrast to his usual formality.

jokes.text: I'm bald!
jokes.setup: Burns apparently loses his wig during the scavenger hunt
jokes.punchline: His shocked announcement of his obvious condition
jokes.explanation: Simple physical gag elevated by Burns' dramatic delivery of obvious information.

jokes.text: It's the boogeyman's grandfather! He's gonna drink our bones!
jokes.setup: Children see Mr. Burns approaching
jokes.punchline: Their specific and creative fears about what he'll do
jokes.explanation: 'Boogeyman's grandfather' and 'drink our bones' are wonderfully specific child fears that feel authentic.

jokes.text: Hey, bar-boy, this table's wobbly. Come jam your foot under it.
jokes.setup: Burns is working as a bartender for Moe
jokes.punchline: Moe's degrading and impractical solution to furniture problems
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Moe's casual cruelty and weird problem-solving.

jokes.text: Hey, bar-boy, write a play where I meet Henry Ford and captain Kirk.
jokes.setup: Moe continues giving Burns demeaning tasks
jokes.punchline: His specific and impossible creative writing assignment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Moe demand that combines historical figure with sci-fi character in typical Moe fashion.

jokes.text: Hey, bar-boy, dance around like an idiot. Like this! I'm so stupid! I'm a little busy. Can I do it later? Sure! You're gonna be all I'm so stupid! I'm so stupid!
jokes.setup: Moe demonstrates the humiliating dance he wants Burns to do
jokes.punchline: Moe's enthusiastic self-deprecation and Burns' attempt to defer
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Moe's self-aware stupidity and Burns' dignified resistance.

jokes.text: Treasure?! Moe has a cappuccino machine?!
jokes.setup: Burns reads about Moe's treasure in the letter
jokes.punchline: Lisa is more shocked by the cappuccino machine detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showing Lisa's priorities and surprise at Moe having fancy equipment.

jokes.text: So to contin... ...ue with the sad sto... ...Ry of Moe's treasure.
jokes.setup: Scene transition back to Lisa telling the story
jokes.punchline: Her words are split awkwardly across scene cuts
jokes.explanation: Clever editing gag that acknowledges the show's scene structure.

jokes.text: She had gossamer hair, limpid eyes and the rack of an angel.
jokes.setup: Moe describes meeting Edna with poetic language
jokes.punchline: The romantic description ends with crude objectification
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe - attempting romance but revealing his true nature. The contrast is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Me? Well... I, for a living? Well, there's, all kinds of things a fella can do, such as hemming and hawing and, but me, I'm a highly respected... therapist for alcoholics.
jokes.setup: Edna asks what Moe does for a living
jokes.punchline: His stammering attempt to lie about his profession
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Moe's terrible lying skills and desperate attempt to impress.

jokes.text: Barney, how do you keep getting back in? I'm a drunk. I don't know nothing about how I do anything.
jokes.setup: Moe tries to keep his regulars out while dating Edna
jokes.punchline: Barney's honest and logical explanation for his mysterious reappearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment - self-aware about his condition but utterly matter-of-fact. Brilliant delivery.

jokes.text: As a result of which, Krabappel was letting me do her.
jokes.setup: Moe explains how his gentlemanly behavior improved his relationship
jokes.punchline: His crude and clinical description of intimacy
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe - turning romantic success into crude bragging. Character consistent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Lab partners don't treat lab partners like this! Shut up and attract lightning.
jokes.setup: Bart complains about being mistreated during an experiment
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dismissive command reveals they're doing something dangerous
jokes.explanation: Great sibling dynamic with Lisa's casual endangerment of Bart for science.

jokes.text: Don't pigeonhole us. We have other vices. I dress up like a baby.
jokes.setup: Alcoholics object to being labeled as one-dimensional
jokes.punchline: Carl's disturbing revelation about his other hobby
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - they want to be seen as complex, but Carl's complexity is disturbing.

jokes.text: Listen, boozebags, I got a good thing going here. If you mess it up, I will out the one of you that is gay.
jokes.setup: Moe threatens his regular customers to maintain his lie
jokes.punchline: His specific blackmail threat about outing someone
jokes.explanation: Dark Moe humor showing his manipulative nature and casual cruelty to his friends.

jokes.text: And if they're interested in wife-swapping, who am I to say no? Hey, I'm just the new guy.
jokes.setup: Moe fantasizes about playing bridge with neighbors in his new life
jokes.punchline: His casual openness to sexual activities with said neighbors
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe escalation from innocent to inappropriate. The 'new guy' excuse is funny.

jokes.text: I totally have a donation for them. Coins money gold!
jokes.setup: Snake asks for directions to the museum
jokes.punchline: His valley-speak description of valuable artifacts
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing young Snake's surfer-dude speech pattern and naive honesty.

jokes.text: could you be any more pre-columbian?
jokes.setup: Snake describes finding ancient Mayan artifacts
jokes.punchline: Uses archaeological terminology in valley-speak phrasing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mashup of valley girl speech with historical terminology. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: You can't donate that gold to the museum today, because, it's closed, so they can clean under the wangs on the statues.
jokes.setup: Moe tries to delay Snake's museum visit
jokes.punchline: His crude and specific lie about museum maintenance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe improvisation - crude but oddly specific detail that sounds plausible enough to work.

jokes.text: Love had handed me an awful dilemma. Should I rob this guy or rob him and kill him? I decided to do the right thing.
jokes.setup: Moe faces a moral choice about the gold situation
jokes.punchline: His 'right thing' choice is still robbery, just without murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy showing Moe's twisted moral compass. The setup makes robbery sound virtuous.

jokes.text: And who would suspect me, professor Jailbird?!
jokes.setup: Snake decides to turn to crime after being robbed
jokes.punchline: His ironic future criminal name while still naive
jokes.explanation: Good origin story joke showing how Snake became his current criminal self.

jokes.text: Back in a flash, my sweet little gargoyle!
jokes.setup: Moe affectionately addresses Edna as she goes into the school
jokes.punchline: His romantic pet name is comparing her to a grotesque architectural feature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moe moment - he thinks he's being romantic but reveals his true perception. Perfectly economical.

jokes.text: But fate likes to play a little game called up yours, Moe.
jokes.setup: Moe was at his happiest point in the relationship
jokes.punchline: His crude personification of fate's cruelty
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe philosophy - turning cosmic concepts into crude personal attacks. Very quotable.

jokes.text: That load of crap? No, I was just keeping her busy while Nelson stole microscopes.
jokes.setup: Bart is questioned about his touching summer detention story
jokes.punchline: Reveals the heartwarming moment was actually a criminal distraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment that subverts the heartwarming teacher-student story with criminal pragmatism.

jokes.text: sweet, misunderstood boys, who just need someone to recognize the basic goodness that's trapped inside them and is desperately trying to get out.
jokes.setup: Edna explains why she stayed to help students like Bart
jokes.punchline: Her sincere belief in Bart's goodness contrasts with his actual criminal behavior
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between Edna's faith and Bart's reality, enhanced by the previous revelation.

jokes.text: I just sat there playing our song on the jukebox, one gold... coin at a time.
jokes.setup: Moe explains how he spent the stolen gold
jokes.punchline: Wasted his fortune on jukebox plays of a romantic song
jokes.explanation: Perfectly pathetic Moe behavior - wasting stolen treasure on self-pity. The pacing is excellent.

jokes.text: I'm obsessive-compulsive! One, two, three, four. No nucular plant without a photo. One, two, three, four.
jokes.setup: Rich Texan explains why he won't return Burns' plant without the photo
jokes.punchline: Reveals his compulsive counting behavior
jokes.explanation: Establishes the Texan's OCD as explanation for his rigid adherence to scavenger hunt rules.

jokes.text: Take me, a barren old thistle, and spare this sweet young flower.
jokes.setup: Burns offers to sacrifice himself for Lisa
jokes.punchline: His unflattering but accurate botanical self-description
jokes.explanation: Good Burns moment with poetic language that's both noble and self-deprecating.

jokes.text: The sheep was no danger at all. I sacrificed my gorgeous body for nothing. This must be what it's like to have a baby.
jokes.setup: Lisa realizes she was wrong about the sheep's intentions
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre comparison of disappointment to childbirth
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Lisa comparison that shows her precocious but warped perspective.

jokes.text: Well, it was very moving, but the thing is, bats are chewing my legs!
jokes.setup: Homer seems emotionally affected by Lisa's story
jokes.punchline: Reveals his tears are from physical pain, not emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer misdirection - appears to show growth but it's just physical discomfort.

jokes.text: Not long ago, it was my day to take care of the baby, so I was out in the forest, hiding.
jokes.setup: Homer explains how he discovered the cave
jokes.punchline: Casually admits he abandons childcare responsibilities to hide in the woods
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting philosophy - complete honesty about his terrible behavior.

jokes.text: I figured he'd never miss two or three pieces... money which we could use to pay for Bart's operation.
jokes.setup: Homer justifies his theft plan
jokes.punchline: Reveals Bart needs an operation (which is news to Bart)
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Bart's surprise reaction and Homer's casual revelation of serious news.

jokes.text: I need an operation? That's a story for another day.
jokes.setup: Bart learns he needs surgery
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses this major revelation as unimportant
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer priorities - treating major medical news as a minor plot thread.

jokes.text: Texas left, which is your 'down.'
jokes.setup: Rich Texan gives directions to the gold location
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical regional directional system
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist Texas character moment - creating a completely illogical regional navigation system.

jokes.text: Coolest entrance gets the gold.
jokes.setup: Multiple criminals arrive to steal the gold
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony proposes settling the dispute based on style points
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of criminal standoff tropes with Fat Tony's aesthetic-based solution.

jokes.text: Is it okay that I brought my son? This is my day with him. Jeremy, there's a boy over there you can play with. Do you like XBox?
jokes.setup: Armed criminal standoff in a cave
jokes.punchline: One criminal brought his kid and arranges a playdate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of criminal activity with mundane parenting responsibilities.

jokes.text: Looks like we got us a mexican standoff.
jokes.setup: Multiple parties pointing guns at each other in the cave
jokes.punchline: The classic standoff terminology applied to the ridiculous situation
jokes.explanation: Standard reference that works in context but isn't particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Moe, will the gold bring back Edna's love? It could. Definitely.
jokes.setup: Lisa questions whether money will solve Moe's romantic problems
jokes.punchline: Moe's increasing confidence in a dubious premise
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's self-deception escalating under pressure.

jokes.text: Lady, I oughta fill you full of... gratitude!
jokes.setup: Fat Tony threatens Lisa aggressively
jokes.punchline: Completes the threat with a positive sentiment
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts Fat Tony's threatening tone with unexpected kindness.

jokes.text: At this point, all I want's a man with a healthy libido.
jokes.setup: Bart questions why Edna is dating Moe
jokes.punchline: Her frank admission about lowered romantic standards
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Edna's pragmatic approach to dating at her age.

jokes.text: Moe can't catch a break! One, two, three, four. Can't catch a break. One, two, three, four.
jokes.setup: Rich Texan sympathizes with Moe's romantic situation
jokes.punchline: His compulsive counting continues even during emotional moments
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the Texan's compulsive behavior that shows it persists even during sympathy.
